name: Sounds 2 Sounds
description: A major South Island crossing with big elevation and remote links.
difficultyExplanation: The route stacks major climbing with remote sections and rapidly changing alpine weather. It rewards experienced riders who can manage long days and limited bailout options.
beAwareOf: Weather changes, wind exposure, and changing trail conditions can all materially affect daily progress.
tips: Start early, carry a weather margin, and keep backup navigation plus an extra food and water buffer.
trickiestSection: The Richmond Range crossing is the crux - sustained climbing, loose trail, and no bailout for extended sections. Weather windows are critical.
routeSummary: Sounds 2 Sounds is a hard bikepacking route covering about 1,499 km, commonly ridden over 10 days. A major South Island crossing with big elevation and remote links. Riders can expect 45% Gravel / 15% Road / 40% Track as the route unfolds.

Good planning makes this route far more enjoyable. The best riding window is generally Oct-Apr, with resupply described as Carry enough food and water for longer gaps between settlements; verify opening hours before committing to remote sections.. Overnight options are typically A mix of camping, huts, and local stays depending on your pace and daily target distance..

The key section to manage is: The Richmond Range crossing is the crux - sustained climbing, loose trail, and no bailout for extended sections. Weather windows are critical. Keep a close eye on Weather changes, wind exposure, and changing trail conditions can all materially affect daily progress., especially if conditions change during the day.
recommendedSetupText: For this 10-day route, we recommend a Hardtail MTB to balance comfort and control across mixed terrain. This setup keeps key gear stable and easy to access over long days.
